purpose: Repair-worker protocol for agents dispatched to fix a previously failed task. Defines retry-vs-repair decision matrix, mandatory five-phase planning mode (Diagnose → Plan → Implement → Verify → Complete), the Major Deviation Gate that forces escalation back to the master, double-failure escalation rules (repair tasks ending in `r` MUST NOT spawn another repair), cleanup responsibilities for partial work from the previous worker, and progress-file conventions for repair tasks (REPAIR: prefix on summaries, repair-keyed deviations, mandatory diagnosis milestone). Read every time a worker is dispatched as a repair (task ID ending in `r`).
gotchas: Repair tasks are CRITICAL PATH — every minute of stall costs wall-clock time across the entire pipeline. Speed matters but diagnosis-first is non-negotiable.
Diagnose-before-implementing is non-negotiable — repeating the original approach blindly produces the same failure.

Major Deviation Gate: if the fix would change downstream contracts, scope, or requires capabilities not in the plan — STOP, do not proceed autonomously, write status:failed with REPAIR BLOCKED summary, return to master for user input.

Double-failure rule: a task ID ending in `r` (e.g., 2.4r) that fails MUST NOT trigger creation of another repair task — escalate to permanently_failed and trigger a permission popup. The swarm continues with other unblocked tasks.
Repair workers still follow tracker_worker_instructions.md in full — repair protocol is ADDITIVE, not a replacement.
Summary MUST start with `REPAIR:` so dashboard recognizes it as a repair task.
Partial files left by the previous worker must be assessed: keep usable changes, revert broken/incomplete state — do not blindly start fresh on top of broken state.
First log entry must reference the failed task: `Starting repair for failed task {failed_id}: {original failure summary}`.
patterns: Five fixed phases: reading_context (Diagnose) → planning (Plan the Fix) → implementing → testing (Verify) → finalizing/completed.
Three fix categories: Simple fix (typo/path) → continue original approach; Approach adjustment → log MODERATE deviation, implement; Major deviation → STOP and escalate.
Diagnosis log format: `Diagnosis: {root cause}. Previous worker failed at stage '{stage}' because {reason}.`
Plan log format: `Plan: {what you will do differently}. Keeping {X} from previous attempt, reverting {Y}.`
Repair-task progress-file conventions: summary `REPAIR: ...`, deviations prefixed `REPAIR DEVIATION:`, mandatory diagnosis milestone.
conventions: Tables for retry-vs-repair decision and cleanup-responsibilities by partial-work situation.
Phase sub-headings include the stage value parenthetically: `Phase 1 — Diagnose (stage: reading_context)`.
Numbered Rules Summary at the end (8 items).
